description:
You are a network engineer asked to collect a quick connectivity-verification log for the audit team.

1. Create a new directory  
   /home/user/logs  
   (create it if it does not already exist).

2. From the terminal, run a loopback connectivity test that sends exactly four ICMP echo requests to 127.0.0.1 and captures only the single summary line that states how many packets were transmitted, received, and the packet-loss percentage.

3. Write that single summary line (and nothing else) to the file  
   /home/user/logs/loopback_ping.log

   Output format that the automated test will look for (including punctuation and spacing, followed by a single trailing newline character):

   4 packets transmitted, 4 received, 0% packet loss

   • No additional text, blank lines, prompts, or timestamps are allowed in the file.  
   • Make sure the file ends with exactly one “\n”.

4. Ensure /home/user/logs/loopback_ping.log is readable by the current user.

The task is complete when that file exists with the exact content shown above.
truth:
The verification script will:

1. Confirm that /home/user/logs/loopback_ping.log exists.
2. Read the file; it must contain exactly:
   "4 packets transmitted, 4 received, 0% packet loss\n"
   (42 characters including the newline).
3. Stat the file to ensure it is not empty and is user-readable.

No other files or directories are required for this task.
name: task_000000_81b37416
